sos: Tap the red SOS button once. Authorities and your emergency contacts are notified instantly with your live location.
zone: You may be near a monitored risk zone. Avoid isolated streets, stay on main roads, and keep GPS active.
safe: Prefer crowded areas, avoid unknown shortcuts, keep phone battery above 30%, share your live location with a trusted contact.
scam: Common scams: overpriced taxis, fake tour guides, distraction theft. Use official transport, verify prices upfront.
night: At night: stick to well-lit main roads, avoid empty alleys, travel in groups, keep emergency contact informed.
night_high: HIGH RISK: Avoid going out at night. If you must, stay on main roads, travel in groups, and keep SOS ready.
help: SafeTrip features: Live location tracking, SOS alerts, Incident reporting, Risk zone map, AI safety chat, Smart alerts.
local: Use registered auto-rickshaws or app-based cabs, avoid displaying expensive items, carry a local SIM.
sms: SMS fallback sends a pre-filled emergency message with your GPS coordinates and Google Maps link - works without internet.
offline: SafeTrip queues your SOS and incident reports when offline and auto-syncs when you reconnect.
tracking: Toggle Share Location on your dashboard. Your live position is shared with safety authorities in real time.
lost: If lost: stay calm, use Google Maps offline, ask a shopkeeper or police for directions.
passport: If passport lost: contact your embassy immediately. File a police report first.
wallet: If wallet stolen: block your cards via your bank app immediately, file a police report.
phone: If phone stolen: use another device to log into SafeTrip. Your blockchain ID is saved in the cloud.
medical: For medical emergency: tap SOS immediately. Dial 108 for ambulance. Government hospitals provide free emergency treatment.
hospital: Dial 108 for ambulance or 112 for national emergency. Tap SOS to alert authorities.
police: Dial 100 for police or 112 for national emergency. Report incidents in SafeTrip.
ambulance: Dial 108 for ambulance anywhere in India. Tap SOS to simultaneously alert local authorities.
fire: Dial 101 for fire emergency or 112 for national emergency. Move away from the building immediately.
flood: Move to higher ground immediately. Avoid walking through floodwater. Tap SOS to alert authorities.
transport: Use Ola, Uber, or Rapido for safe rides. Always verify driver name and vehicle number.
taxi: Use app-based cabs (Ola/Uber) - they are tracked and safer. Avoid unmarked taxis.
train: Keep your ticket and ID handy. Use Railway Police helpline 1800-111-322 for emergencies.
bus: Use government or reputed private buses. Keep your bag in front of you.
food: Prefer restaurants with visible kitchens and good ratings. Carry ORS sachets for dehydration.
water: Drink only bottled or filtered water. Stay hydrated especially in summer.
weather: Check weather before going out. Carry an umbrella during monsoon.
heat: Stay hydrated, wear light clothing, avoid going out between 12pm-4pm in summer.
atm: Use ATMs inside banks or malls. Cover the keypad while entering PIN.
money: Carry limited cash. Use UPI apps (GPay, PhonePe, Paytm) for payments.
hotel: Verify your hotel booking before arrival. Keep the hotel address saved offline.
language: Use Google Translate offline mode for language barriers. Most urban areas have English speakers.
wifi: Avoid public WiFi for banking. Use your mobile data or a VPN.
battery: Keep your phone charged above 30% at all times. Carry a power bank.
emergency: Emergency numbers: Police 100, Ambulance 108, Fire 101, Women helpline 1091, National 112.
numbers: Emergency numbers: Police 100, Ambulance 108, Fire 101, Women helpline 1091, National 112.
women: Women safety: travel in groups, use women-only compartments in trains, dial 1091 for women helpline.
theft: If something stolen: file an FIR at the nearest police station immediately.
harassment: If harassed: move to a crowded area, call 100 or 1091, use the SOS button.
accident: Road accident: call 108 for ambulance, 100 for police. Do not move injured persons.
currency: Indian currency is the Rupee (INR). Use authorized money changers or banks.
sim: Buy a local SIM at the airport or a registered telecom store. Jio and Airtel offer affordable plans.
id: Your Digital Safety ID (BLK-XXXXXXXX) is your unique SafeTrip identity. Show the QR code to authorities.
predict: SafeTrip predicts your risk before it becomes critical. When predicted score crosses 85, you get an early warning.
report: To report an incident: tap Report Incident, select type and severity, add location and description.
greeting: Hello! I am your SafeTrip AI safety assistant. Ask me about your risk score, safety tips, SOS, or anything about your trip.
thanks: You are welcome! Stay safe and keep your location tracking ON. Tap SOS anytime you feel unsafe.
default: Stay alert. Risk level: {l} (score: {s}). Keep live tracking ON and avoid low-visibility areas.
